Design Presentation · 2026

Cazateatro Bilingual Theatre Group

LATIN FEST

901

Visual Identity & Event Design

September 26, 2026 · Overton Square · Memphis, TN
Scroll
The Event

A festival for the whole community

Latin Fest 901 is Cazateatro Bilingual Theatre Group's annual celebration — a free, open-to-all community event held at Overton Square in Memphis. Since its founding in 2019, the festival has grown into a gathering space for cultural exchange, joy, and Latin American pride during Hispanic Heritage Month.

In 2026, the festival returns on September 26th, featuring food vendors, clothing, flowers, community organizations, local businesses, artistic performances, and live music. Admission is completely free.

Main Poster

Official Design
Latin Fest 901 · 2026

The poster is the visual heart of the campaign. The central illustration depicts a scene of collective celebration — people from diverse backgrounds united through music, movement, and shared joy.

The diagonal composition creates energy and movement, while the warm palette anchors the design in the spirit of the festival. Termina Black in navy delivers maximum legibility and authority without competing with the illustration.

Primary Font

Termina Black

Secondary Font

Termina Extra Light

Brand Font

Briller

Event Date

September 26, 2026

Venue

Overton Square, Memphis

Admission

Free — Entrada Gratuita

Color Palette

The colors of the festival

The Latin Fest 901 palette was built to communicate energy, warmth, and contemporaneity — without relying on folkloric clichés. Each color has a specific role within the visual system.

Festival Yellow

#F5A500

Night Blue

#1A2A4A

Cazateatro Teal

#2ABFBF

Accent Orange

#E8601C

Festival Yellow · #F5A500

The dominant background color. This golden yellow evokes sunlight, joy, and energy without being aggressive. It is the color that unifies the entire composition — from the background to the illustration accents. It communicates celebration and openness.

Night Blue · #1A2A4A

The primary typographic color. Deep navy ensures maximum contrast against the yellow and creates typographic cohesion across the poster. It appears in the "LATIN FEST" title, "2026," and "SEPTEMBER 26" — unifying the entire visual system.

Cazateatro Teal · #2ABFBF

Cazateatro's brand color, present in the decorative rules of the bottom bar and in illustration details. The teal visually connects the event to the organization's institutional identity.

Accent Orange · #E8601C

An accent color present in the illustration and character details. Orange adds warmth and visual dynamism without dominating the composition. It complements the yellow without competing with it.

Typography

A unified type system

The poster's type system uses three typefaces with clear, differentiated roles — all coexisting within a coherent visual hierarchy.

Aa

Primary Headline

Termina Black

LATIN FEST · SEPTEMBER 26 — Maximum presence and legibility. Contemporary geometric sans-serif.

Aa

Secondary Text

Termina Extra Light

SAVE THE DATE — Weight contrast with Black. Elegant and understated.

Aa

Brand / Identity

Briller

Cazateatro · CAZATEATRO.ORG · 2026 — Connects to Cazateatro's established brand identity.

Illustration & Characters

A celebration that represents us

The poster's central illustration comes from a place of cultural honesty. Yes, Latin Americans celebrate with music and dance — but these characters have dignity, genuine expression, and authentic diversity. They are not caricatures or empty stereotypes. They are us.

Beyond the main poster, the campaign includes an additional illustration library to be used across digital outreach and social media — each piece capturing different moments and groups from the festival experience.

Friends

The Dancers

The Girls

The Union

Happiness

The Kids

The Dog

Celebrating

Latin Fest 901
